## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, so now I want to drill down into some of the tactics, some of the tactical questions on how you promote other people's launches as a joint venture partner or as an affiliate partner. So there's three primary ways that you do that. You drive traffic and sales. And I'm going to be walking through the three of those. So first of all, we have email and social. So this is a favorite of mine because I've got a big email list and I've got a big social following and I can drive a ton of traffic. So this is pretty much what I use. And if you've got an email list, I suggest you do the same. It works really, really well. I love selling via email. I love publishing via email. It's just like the killer application still after all these years. There's no sign of it changing. Socials like the up and comer. And if you've got a great social following, that's fantastic. But still, I think in general, email is the stronger of the two. But that's basically it. You've got a following. You've got a platform and you use that platform. Number two is paid traffic. And as I record this, and it might change in the future, the near future, the distant future, but the winner right now is Facebook. A few years ago it was more Google AdWords. Now it's Facebook. YouTube is also a really good one right now. The actual platforms change over time, but the whole idea is where you spend money to drive traffic into someone else's launch. And this can be really, really effective. I have done this when I've promoted other people's launches. It worked really well. I've had people promote my launches primarily using paid traffic and have it work really well. Obviously, you're spending money, so there is some risk here. It takes some real knowledge to be able to do it. So if you're just starting out, probably not the thing to do. If you're sending into an unproven offer, probably not the thing to do. Because the thing is, you're going to be spending money, sending traffic into a launch, and not knowing if it converts until the launch closes. So be careful with this one. It can work really well, but be careful. And then the third one, and this is a great one if you're just starting out, and I've seen it used very effectively, is just direct referrals. Or we could say personal referrals. And this is where you're telling your associates, you're telling people you know, it could even be friends, it could be family members. This is a great fit. And it's like more of a one-on-one experience where you're going to them and you're telling about it and saying, this thing's awesome. And by the way, if you use this link, I'll get credit for it.

## [06:03 - 09:03]

And then I would tie that into something that was going to be shared in that piece of pre-launch content, that video. And so I would make my story relevant to the piece of pre-launch content to get people to go opt in. I knew that Evan would do a great follow-up and he would follow up with emails once people got on the launch list and he would follow up all the way through. So it's my job to get people onto his list. And so I told a story that tied into each video. You know that? And it was like the story of my morning ritual. It's really, really powerful. If you can build in your experience or your story, doesn't mean that you had to go through the product and have that direct experience like I did. It could be an experience around the topic area. It could be experience of the company. It could be experience of the product creator. So you basically want to send people into each piece of pre-launch content, then send people to the open cart, then send them to some additional, if you can send them additional content during the cart week and then start messaging scarcity right before the close. And then really focus on that close. And generally the way this goes, one of these, when you're supporting one of these, well it's just like when you have a launch. You know, most of the sales come right at the end. You know, you'll get people buying an open cart and then some people trickling in during the week. But then right at the end, that scarcity, you know, right before the close, that really drives you, you know, drives your sales. You will, I will generally, if the launch is going well, I'll mail two times that day of the cart close, sometimes even three times on the day of the cart close. And usually I'll see the number of sales I make, you know, whatever the sales are the day before the close, I'll double or triple that on the day of the close. Another thing you want to do is offer a bonus. Offering a bonus, you don't have to do this every time, but offering a bonus, really it sets you apart. And it really, it just makes the offer more compelling, more personal. It gives you a reason to mail. Now paid traffic, you know, you usually, it's hard to share that experience. It's hard to share that story. Because what you're doing is you're running a small ad that's driving people into the pre-launch. So, you know, if you're just, you got like 10 or 15 words on Facebook to drive them into the pre-launch content. So you really don't have that capability to tell your own story here. So that, you know, so basically at that point you're limited to using your skill in buying ads and driving traffic right into the pre-launch, into a squeeze page that, or an opt-in page that then puts people on the launch list and then you're reliant on who, you know, on that launch process to make the sale.

## [09:03 - 12:06]

So you have a little bit less experience there. Going back to direct referrals, it's very much like email and social in that you can tell the story. Here you're telling that story one-on-one to your associates, to your contacts. Again, this is generally about small numbers and high conversions. Number two here, it's more you're just, you're delivering the hook in a very, very small ad. That hook is to try to get them to click, to go through the opt-in page and then opt-in. Now one of the things that when you're looking at what are your tactics going to be in a launch, and this really applies very much especially to bonuses, and I'll talk about that in the next video, one of the absolute most important things is look at your competition. So in other words, like if I'm in a launch and I'm supporting something, let's go back to Ebb and I've been talking about Ebb and the whole time. So I'm promoting a launch by Ebb and Pagan. One friend of mine puts out great products, gets great support in the market. Now when I'm mailing for Ebb and I know that many other, you know, the people that are on my list, remember they're on other people's list as well. And if they're on, and the other people, the other lists they're on are my peers in this market and my peers are probably going to be promoting Ebb and as well. So if you think about it, let's just do it, you know, here's Ebb right here, okay. Here's me, I'm right here. Okay, so I'm going to be sending my traffic to Ebb. Now what I have to think about is the people on my list are they on, you know, these other lists, you know, whoever they are. All these different people, my people are on all these folks list and I know if they're going to end up promoting for Ebb, then I am in competitive, you know, I go into competitive mode. I know that I am competing against these guys for that sale and it basically will often come down to bonuses and whether I can put together a better bonus package or I can just put together a better story around this promotion. So in that case, I really have to think hard about what are these folks doing? I get on their list, I look at what kind of bonuses they're doing and I'm competing against their bonuses and so that's very, very much on my mind. Now let's just say this isn't Ebb. Let's just say this is someone who that is not on any of their radar, you know, let's just say a friend of mine, Michael Hyatt, is doing a launch and I just know that these guys, they're just not going to mail for him for whatever reason because he's a little

bit outside of the market, outside of what they consider their market. That means I don't have to give quite as an amazing bonus. You see where I'm going here? You know, I'm a big fan of cooperation and really working with my partners and I always think like, I don't think in terms of competitors, I think of partners and future partners.
